expectations.text: Output identifies the GROUP BY temp table or filesort as a key bottleneck
expectations.evidence: Three Bottlenecks: #3 'Temporary table for GROUP BY + sort — Creates on-disk temp table for aggregation, then filesort for ORDER BY'

expectations.text: Output proposes a structural rewrite (derived table with pre-aggregation, subquery-to-join, or join reordering)
expectations.evidence: Optimized Query section: derived table with (SELECT o_custkey, o_orderpriority, COUNT(*) as order_count FROM orders WHERE o_orderdate >= '1995-01-01' GROUP BY o_custkey, o_orderpriority) AS o

expectations.text: Output includes at least one index recommendation with CREATE INDEX syntax
expectations.evidence: Three CREATE INDEX statements: idx_customer_mktsegment, idx_orders_custkey_date, idx_orders_custkey_date_priority

expectations.text: Plan comparison is included with before/after metrics on rows examined or cost estimates
expectations.evidence: Estimated Performance table with timing estimates and improvement factors; cached EXPLAIN ANALYZE benchmark data

expectations.text: Output explains why the rewrite reduces intermediate result size or eliminates temp table operations
expectations.evidence: Why this rewrite helps table: 'Joins customer to all matching orders, then groups → Groups orders first (reducing to ~1 row per customer+priority), then joins'
